steps.title: Identify the shared observation
steps.content:
Both authors explicitly acknowledge that Capital and Ideology differs structurally from Piketty's earlier work.

Text 1 contrasts the “more rigorous structure” of the first book with the second book, while Text 2 states that “Capital and Ideology has different aims than Piketty's earlier books.”

steps.title: Match the answer
steps.content:
Both authors establish that Capital and Ideology is structurally different from his earlier work—that's why choice A is correct.

Text 1 judges this as a flaw (tedious, muddying reasoning), Text 2 as a deliberate choice, but they don't contest the difference itself.

The other three require agreement on substantive matters the texts don't align on—the merit of the digressions, the ranking of the books, or unsupported claims about influence.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
